video_title: Devil May Cry | "Afterlife" Official Music Video feat. Evanescence | Netflix
comment_text:
Native speakers of American English, could you help me, please? In this song, Amy Lee doesn't pronounce the /r/ sound where it's supposed to be in her American accent: 

0:21 - 'before' (no /r/ at the end), 
0:27 - 'over' (just a hint of /r/), 
0:36 - 'your' (no /r/),
and, I think, everywhere else.

Could you explain why? Do American singers often drop /r/ after the vowels when singing? In my (very limited) experience, it's the first case of this kind.

And 0:30 - 'coming' with a rounded sound /o/, resembling a British /o/ in words like 'pot', 'drop', 'boss'. Some mannerism?

Thank you in advance!

video_title: Devil May Cry | "Afterlife" Official Music Video feat. Evanescence | Netflix
comment_text:
Netflix really nailed the nostalgic aspect and the sound design, but failed to portray Dante as he truly is. You know what I'm talking about—the guy who drinks Jack Daniel’s whiskey, is imposing, extremely powerful, and feared. He should never be embarrassed like a silly boy, humiliated at times by a secondary character like Lady. This is truly unacceptable. Netflix has the opportunity to fix this, simply by bringing back the essence of the most badass character of the 2000s in the next episodes! Please, this is the least that fans expect—Dante must reclaim his true protagonism.
Let me know if you'd like any tweaks to the translation! 🔥

video_title: Devil May Cry | "Afterlife" Official Music Video feat. Evanescence | Netflix
comment_text:
This song is heartbreaking. Amy has sang about life and death for so many years that I would've thought by now, she would have sought out God. This song's lyrics are schizophrenic and try to normalize and "cool down" the afterlife of judgement in Hell for sinners. I've always felt Amy's pain in her music and I can see it in her eyes, and although I listen to some of her songs, I see and sing them through the lens of Life eternal in Jesus Messiah. I wish Amy and many like her would trust in Jesus to save them and lead their lives in righteousness, truth, love, and peace. No one is too far gone from the love of God. If you still have breath in your lungs and you're not in a brain-dead condition, you can still call out to Jesus Messiah and ask Him to save you from the impending judgement of your sin debt. If you trust in Jesus Messiah alone, as who He said He is (Yahweh God incarnate); what He did (He was sent into the world, He lived a sinless life, preformed many miracles and fulfilled specific prophecies including the one where He was predestined to give Himself up as the perfect, unblemished sacrificial Lamb to be the propitiation for the sins of the world, including your sins; He died, was buried, took our sins to Hell, defeated death and rose from the grave, and ascended into heaven where He sits at the right hand of God the Father interceeding for us. Why does any of that matter? Because the God is clear when He tells us twice in His Holy Word, that without the shedding of blood, there is no forgiveness of sins. 

If you believe in your heart that this information is true, and you confess with your mouth that Jesus is Lord (to confess in the original Greek, in which it was written, means to covenant with your mouth), then you shall be saved from God's Judgement (aka Hell). If you believe the gospel message of Jesus Messiah and you confess that Jesus is Lord: pray (talk either in your head or out loud) to YHWH God and tell Him you trust that what He did for you in giving Jesus up for you, and that you accept Jesus' sacrifice on the cross for your sins. Thank God for His gift of grace that you cannot earn nor do you deserve because only Jesus is righteous, and ask Him to fill you with His Holy Spirit. Lastly, ask Jesus to make a change in you and to lead you as Lord of your life to the glory of God the Father. 

May YHWH bless all who read this, and may all who  read this first consider it in your brain and then again in your heart.
